items.title: 2026 Federal Utility Partnership Working Group (FUPWG) Seminar
items.content_text: The Federal Utility Partnership Working Group (FUPWG) Seminar meets to share best practices, success stories, case studies, information about Federal Energy Management Program (FEMP) activities, and other business.

items.title: 2026 Electromagnetic Transient (EMT) Simulation Workshop
items.content_text: NERC, in collaboration with OE and ORNL, will host the 2026 Electromagnetic Transient (EMT) Simulation Workshop on Aug. 18-21, 2026, in Washington, D.C. Researchers, consultants, utilities, system operators, and reliability organizations will explore advancements in EMT simulations, including modeling, simulation techniques, input data structure, and automation tools to support evolving industry needs. This workshop will also feature hands-on tutorials on EMT simulation that is supported by DOE i2x.

items.title: SRP to propose mixed-resource project to its board in September
items.content_text: The Marigold Energy Center, about 45 miles south of Phoenix, would include 400 MW of battery storage, 600 MW of solar generation, up to 675 MW of gas generation and a new substation.

items.title: CO2 emissions from US power sector rose 4% last year
items.content_text: The rise in emissions was linked to increased electricity generation and coal use, the U.S. Energy Information Administration said.

items.title: 3 practical steps to ensure the US industrial boom runs on electricity
items.content_text: Long-term electricity procurement, on-site generation, efficiency and flexibility can give manufacturers more ways to manage their energy bills, writes the World Wildlife Fund’s Cihang Yuan.

items.title: US data center electricity use could more than double by 2030: report
items.content_text: Policy responses “reflect a tension between furthering state-level economic development interests and balancing local community health and environmental concerns,” the Kansas Health Institute report states.

items.title: FERC fails to shield PJM consumers from data center transmission costs: ratepayer advocates
items.content_text: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission’s large load “show cause” order for the PJM Interconnection fails to address cost allocation of network upgrades, advocates from five states said.

items.title: $215B in annual energy cost savings starts with heat pumps: ACEEE
items.content_text: Building and other electric efficiency measures would reduce growing peak demand by 20% on average through 2050, according to the American Council for an Energy-Efficient Economy.

items.title: Best Buy adds more solar generation to portfolio
items.content_text: The new solar sites include the retailer’s first store with a rooftop “community solar garden” as it looks to a 2040 net-zero target.

items.title: Data centers drove $6.3B in PJM capacity auction costs: market monitor
items.content_text: In the PJM’s last four capacity auctions, data center-related capacity charges were nearly half of the $63.6 billion in total charges, Monitoring Analytics says.

items.title: We don’t need more proof that flexibility works. We need more people using it.
items.content_text: "Flexibility works. The real bottleneck is scaling participation fast enough to matter."

items.title: AI data center growth could force US utilities to rethink generation plans, BofA says
items.content_text: Data center demand will outpace planned utility capacity additions by more than 100 GW through 2030, increasing reliance on on-site gas generation and battery storage, Bank of America analysts project.
